title: 10 best herbal tinctures for inflamed mouth
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for inflamed mouth are natural liquid extracts made from plants, roots, and herbs that have been used for centuries to soothe and calm inflamed oral tissues. These tinctures work by reducing swelling, inflammation, and pain associated with conditions such as gingivitis, periodontitis, and mouth ulcers. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with inflamed mouth include calendula, chamomile, and peppermint, which can be taken sublingually or added to water for a topical rinse. This natural approach can greatly improve lives by reducing discomfort and promoting overall oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The tannins and flavonoids present in pot marigold work together to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with oral inflammation. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of pot marigold help combat bacterial and viral infections that can contribute to inflamed mouth conditions. As a result, pot marigold tinctures provide natural relief from mouth sores, gum inflammation, and other painful oral issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, which can alleviate symptoms of gum disease and mouth sores.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in pot marigold have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, mouth ulcers, and other oral inflammatory conditions.

Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold's antimicrobial compounds can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that contribute to gum disease, bad breath, and mouth sores, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with an inflamed mouth.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in treating mouth infections and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them in a warm place for several hours.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks shaking daily to extract essence.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container discarding solids and reserve liquid.
Transfer the resulting tincture to small dark bottles and store in refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can provide fast and effective relief from mouth sores, gum inflammation, and oral mucositis. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation by blocking the production of inflammatory enzymes, while also soothing irritated tissues and promoting healing. As a natural remedy, herbal licorice tinctures can be used topically or taken orally to alleviate pain, discomfort, and swelling associated with inflamed mouth conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes in the mouth.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins present in licorice root have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and eliminate harmful bacteria, fungi, or viruses that may be contributing to oral inflammation.

Mucilage content: Licorice root contains a type of fiber called mucilage, which forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by inflamed mouth tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe irritated mucous membranes.
Barks: Licorice bark contains compounds like glycyrin and liquiritigenin, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning for optimal potency.
Grind 10 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 250 milliliters of vodka and the ground root powder in a clean glass jar, ensuring the powder is completely submerged.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction for optimal potency.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting liquid in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like hypericin and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth. When used as a tincture, St john's wort can be applied directly to the affected area, providing quick relief from discomfort and pain caused by inflamed gums, tongue, or cheeks. Its antimicrobial properties also help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, soothing sore gums and reducing pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The flavonoids and hyperforin present in St. John's Wort tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with inflamed mouths.

Antimicrobial properties: St. John's Wort has antimicrobial activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can help combat oral infections and promote healing of wounds and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known to reduce inflammation and soothe mucous membranes.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating inflamed mouth tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers measuring about 100 grams for the preparation.
Combine harvested herbs with high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar filling it up to the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place allowing it to steep for six weeks and one day.
Strain the tincture mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the filtered tincture to smaller dark-tinted glass bottles filling them up and labeling them with the preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the plant's active compounds such as ursolic acid and isothiocyanates. These bioactive molecules effectively reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the mouth, providing fast relief from conditions like gingivitis, periodontitis, and oral ulcers. Additionally, the tincture's antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections that can contribute to chronic inflammation, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamic acid and chicoric acid, help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, alleviating discomfort and inflammation.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and phenolic acids present in Kansas Coneflower have antimicrobial effects, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause oral infections, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.

Immunomodulatory properties: Echinacea's immunomodulatory compounds help regulate the immune response, enhancing the body's natural defense against pathogens and reducing inflammation in the mouth, thereby promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with an inflamed mouth.
Fruits: Containing antiseptic properties, fruits aid in preventing infection and promoting healing of mouth sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots or purchase dried roots from a reputable supplier.
Dry the roots in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least 7 days or until completely dry.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 80% vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place for 6 weeks to allow for infusion and tincture formation.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive compounds present in dandelion, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenoids, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain associated with mouth inflammation. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of dandelion tincture help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to mouth inflammation. By using herbal dandelion tinctures, individuals may experience relief from inflamed mouth symptoms, promoting a healthier oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains bioactive compounds like taraxasterol and triterpenoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in various tissues, including oral mucosa, thereby helping to alleviate swollen and painful mouth tissue.

Antimicrobial properties: Dandelion has been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause oral infections and inflamed mouths, helping to combat the underlying infection and promote healing.

Soothing and protective agents: The tannins and flavonoids present in dandelion have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bitter compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the mouth.
Fruits: The fruits, also known as achenes, contain anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated mucous membranes in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the plant material gently with pure water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Mix 50 grams of chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka and let it macerate for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The herb's soothing effects calm irritated tissues, promoting a healthy balance in the mouth. Additionally, lemon balm's antibacterial qualities help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to inflammation. As a result, lemon balm tinctures provide swift relief from soreness, redness, and pain associated with inflamed mouth, making it an effective natural remedy for maintaining oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the mouth by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The terpenes and phenolic acids present in lemon balm tinctures have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with inflamed mouths.

Antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial compounds, including citral and geraniol, can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to mouth inflammation, such as gum disease or oral thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have a calming effect, which can help soothe and reduce inflammation in the mouth.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly chop 200g of the harvested plant material into smaller pieces for processing.
Combine chopped plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow infusion and extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herb's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce swelling and alleviate pain caused by inflammation. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial agents help combat bacteria and viruses that can contribute to oral infections and inflammation. By incorporating chamomile tincture into a daily routine, individuals with inflamed mouth can experience rapid relief from discomfort and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, alleviating symptoms such as swelling and pain.

Softering and anti-irritant properties: The mucilages (gummy substances) present in chamomile tinctures soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with inflamed tissues.

Calming and sedative effects: Chamomile's calming compounds, such as apigenin, can help to relax the jaw muscles and reduce stress-related tension, which can contribute to or exacerbate inflamed mouth issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots help soothe and calm inflammation in the mouth.
Flowers: With their high content of apigenin and other flavonoids, chamomile flowers provide a gentle and effective antiseptic properties to treat mouth infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Combine one part of matricaria chamomilla flowers with two parts of high-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow the herbs to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label with ingredients dosage and preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, inhibits pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, turmeric's antibacterial properties help combat infections that can cause oral inflammation. As a result, regular use of herbal turmeric tinctures may provide relief from inflamed mouth, gum disease, and other oral health issues. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's curcumin compound reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which helps alleviate swelling and pain in the mouth.
Antibacterial property: Turmeric's essential oils have antibacterial properties that help combat oral bacteria, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing in inflamed areas.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage mouth tissues, helping to reduce oxidative stress and promote overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing powerful bioactive compounds like curcumin, rhyzomes have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and treating mouth infections.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, turmeric roots have been traditionally used to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum diseases and mouth sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes wash them thoroughly and dry completely.
Chop the dried curcuma longa into small pieces then add 4 ounces of 190-proof vodka as a menstruum.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6 weeks shaking daily to extract active compounds from herbs.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids and transfer liquid to dark glass bottles.
Store the final tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years using 30-40 drops per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which directly target the source of the discomfort. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, providing quick relief from symptoms like redness, soreness, and burning sensations. Additionally, ginger's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties help combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the inflammation, promoting a healthy mouth environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal ginger tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed mouth, making it a natural remedy for conditions like gingivitis and periodontitis.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in herbal ginger tincture help protect oral tissues from free radical damage, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to mouth inflammation.

Pain-relieving: Herbal ginger tincture's analgesic properties provide natural relief from pain and discomfort caused by inflamed mouth, making it a useful treatment for conditions like aphthous ulcers and dry socket.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm inflamed mouths.
Rhizomes: Contain volatile oils that help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with oral mucositis, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for mouth sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger root, cleaning and cutting it into small pieces for optimal extraction.
Combine the cut ginger pieces with vodka at a ratio of one part ginger to three parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily, to facilitate optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Transfer the resulting herbal tincture to small dark-tinted glass bottles, labeling them with date of preparation and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Poppy tinctures helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The plant-based extract contains a wealth of natural compounds, including salicylic acid, which is known to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the oral cavity. Additionally, poppy tincture has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to inflamed mouth conditions. By applying it topically, individuals can experience rapid relief from discomfort, soreness, and inflammation, promoting a healthier and more comfortable mouth.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Poppy contains compounds like salicin and flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the body, which could potentially help soothe inflamed mouth tissues.
Pain relief: Poppy contains alkaloids like morphine and codeine, which can provide natural pain relief by binding to opioid receptors in the brain, reducing discomfort and inflammation in the mouth.

Antioxidant properties: Poppy contains antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage caused by chronic inflammation, potentially reducing the severity of inflamed mouth conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, including morphine and codeine, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth.
Bark: Containing salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which reduces swelling and eases discomfort associated with inflamed mouth tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried papaver rhoeas flowers in amounts of one part herb to four parts vodka.
Combine the harvested herb and vodka mixture in a clean glass jar with a lid and store it for six weeks.
Strain the liquid from the jar through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dark place for one year.
Use two to four milliliters of the tincture three times daily mixed with water or as directed by a healthcare provider.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat an inflamed mouth with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of Calendula, Echinacea, and Goldenseal. Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, while Echinacea boosts the immune system to combat infection. Goldenseal's antibacterial properties prevent further bacterial growth. Mix 5-7 drops of each tincture in water and swish it around your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out three times a day. Repeat as needed until symptoms subside.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed mouth that are treated with herbal tinctures are various oral and throat issues. These include gum inflammation, tooth abscesses, tonsillitis, and thrush. Herbal tinctures such as goldenseal, echinacea, and myrrh have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal irritated mucous membranes in the mouth and throat. Additionally, tinctures like calendula and tea tree oil may help reduce inflammation and combat infection.
